Dental Assistant License in North Dakota

Here's what you need to know.

Last updated 2026-03-22 · Sources verified

At a glance

Endorsement fee
$50
Processing time
2 to 4 Weeks
Renewal fee
$50
Fingerprinting
Required
Board
North Dakota State Board of Dental Examiners
Path
Endorsement through North Dakota State Board of Dental Examiners
See the steps Primary board page

Last updated 2026-03-22.

Out-of-State Dental Assistants

There is no active interstate compact route for dental assistant licensing in North Dakota. Plan on endorsement through North Dakota State Board of Dental Examiners.

Key Timeline Factors

Keep these requirements in mind, as they are the most likely to impact your processing timeline in North Dakota.

  • Fingerprinting: Required. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail. Completing prints outside North Dakota can add 2-4 weeks.
  • License verification: North Dakota requires verification of your current out-of-state license before your file is complete.
  • Compact status: No active compact route here. Plan on endorsement.
  • Temporary license: The board lists a temporary or provisional route while the full file is pending.

North Dakota Endorsement Requirements

This is the step-by-step endorsement checklist for already-licensed dental assistants applying to practice in North Dakota.

  1. Step 1

    Submit Your Application

    Apply through the North Dakota State Board of Dental Examiners. The application fee is $50.

  2. Step 2

    Provide License Verification

    North Dakota requires official verification of your active out-of-state license. This must be sent directly to the board from your current state board or its official online verification system before your file can be considered complete.

  3. Step 3

    Provide Proof of Training

    Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.

  4. Step 4

    Provide DANB Certification

    Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.

  5. Step 5

    Provide Radiography Certification

    Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

  6. Step 6

    Complete Fingerprinting and Background Screening

    North Dakota requires a criminal background check for all applicants. Out-of-state applicants can use IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan OR FD-258 hard card via mail. Completing prints outside North Dakota can add 2-4 weeks to your processing time.

  7. Step 7

    Await Board Processing

    Plan for a processing timeline of about 2 to 4 Weeks once your file is complete. If you need to start working immediately, the board offers a temporary or provisional licensing route that you can utilize while your full application is pending.

  8. Step 8

    Apply for State Credential

    Submit application to the North Dakota State Board of Dental Examiners with required documentation and fees.

Fees, Processing Time, and Renewal

Here is a complete breakdown of the costs and timelines associated with getting and maintaining your North Dakota Dental Assistant license.

Initial Licensing Costs

Application fee
$50
Background check
Varies by provider
Total estimated cost
$50 (Covers your application fee plus the initial license issuance fee)

Processing Timeline

Estimated time
2 to 4 Weeks (once your application and fingerprinting are fully received by the board)

Ongoing License Renewal

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$50

Temporary License

North Dakota offers a temporary or provisional license so you can start working while your full endorsement application is processing.

Board
North Dakota State Board of Dental Examiners
Department
State Board of Dentistry
Phone
(701) 258-8600

Source: Primary board website

North Dakota Dental Assistant Licensing FAQ

Does North Dakota require a dental assistant license?

Yes, North Dakota requires Registered Dental Assistant through the state board.

Is DANB certification recognized in North Dakota?

Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in North Dakota.

Can I take dental x-rays in North Dakota?

Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in North Dakota?

State application fees are $50. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.
